task: I need to send a short note to a client about a delay, and I want to make sure it covers the key point without creating extra back-and-forth. Can you help me draft a simple message that gets the update across clearly?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a simple, clear note you can send:

**Subject:** Update on Timing

Hi [Client Name],  
I wanted to let you know that there’s been a slight delay on our side, and the timeline will need to shift accordingly. We’re working to get everything back on track and will keep you updated as soon as we have a revised delivery date. Thanks for your patience and understanding.

Best,  
[Your Name]

If you want, I can also make this **more formal, more apologetic, or shorter**.

user_simulator.text:
Fine, but it’s a little vague. I’d want it to say what changed without sounding slippery. Something like:

Hi [Client Name],

Quick update: I’m running behind on this one due to [brief reason]. I’m working to get it finished and will send you the revised delivery time by [day/time].

Best,
[Your Name]

That keeps it clean. If you want, make it even tighter, or a bit more formal.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a tighter version that stays clear without overexplaining:

Hi [Client Name],

Quick update: I’m running behind on this due to [brief reason]. I’m working to get it finished and will send you the revised delivery time by [day/time].

Best,  
[Your Name]

If you want, I can also make it:
- a little more polished,
- more apologetic, or
- even shorter.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. Clear enough, and it doesn’t sound evasive.
